name: foundrytools-cli

metadata.name: foundrytools-cli
metadata.summary: A set of command line tools to inspect, manipulate and convert font files

documentation:
# FoundryTools-CLI
FoundryTools-CLI, former known as ftCLI, is a collection of command line tools written in Python to inspect, manipulate
and convert fonts. It takes advantage of the capabilities made available by other tools such as:

* [FontTools](https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools)
* [AFDKO](https://github.com/adobe-type-tools/afdko)
* [skia-pathops](https://github.com/fonttools/skia-pathops)
* [cffsubr](https://github.com/adobe-type-tools/cffsubr)
* [ttfautohint-py](https://github.com/fonttools/ttfautohint-py)
* [dehinter](https://github.com/source-foundry/dehinter)

![image](https://github.com/ftCLI/FoundryTools-CLI/assets/83063506/f648829c-3e3c-45ee-be3e-b5f9b35b30f7)

The command line interface is built with [click](https://github.com/pallets/click/) and tables are rendered by
[rich](https://github.com/Textualize/rich).

Even if not directly imported to keep the footprint as small as possible, portions of code have been copied from
[fontbakery](https://github.com/googlefonts/fontbakery) and [font-line](https://github.com/source-foundry/font-line).

## Installation
FoundryTools-CLI requires Python 3.9 or later.

**Note for Windows users**: installation on Python 3.11 and later may fail due to an error while building the wheel for
the `AFDKO` dependency. This is a known issue and a workaround is to install Visual Studio 2022 (Community Edition is
fine) and the `Python development` workload, selecting the `Python native development tools` optional component.

![image](https://github.com/ftCLI/FoundryTools-CLI/assets/83063506/3dcb4f74-bb94-4634-b28a-b6aeba7d8575)


**IMPORTANT**: If you have Python 2.x installed in your system, you may have to use `python3` (instead of `python`) in
the commands below.

### pip
FoundryTools-CLI releases are available on the Python Package Index (PyPI), so it can be installed with
[pip](https://pip.pypa.io/):

    python -m pip install foundrytools-cli

### Editable mode
If you would like to contribute to the development, you can clone the repository from GitHub, install the package in
'editable' mode and modify the source code in place. We strongly recommend using a virtual environment.

    # clone the repository:
    git clone https://github.com/ftCLI/foundrytools-cli.git
    cd foundrytools-cli

    # create new virtual environment named e.g. ftcli-venv, or whatever you prefer:
    python -m venv ftcli-venv
    
    # to activate the virtual environmtnet in macOS and Linux, do:
    . ftcli-venv/bin/activate
    
    # to activate the virtual environment in Windows, do:
    ftcli-venv\Scripts\activate.bat
    
    # install in 'editable' mode
    python -m pip install -e .


## Documentation
FoundryTools-CLI is a Terminal app where commands are logically organized into subcommands.

Please refer to the [user documentation](https://ftcli.github.io/FoundryTools-CLI/).

## License
FoundryTools-CLI is available under the [MIT license](LICENSE)

## Credits
To Sergiev. May You rest in peace.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_print.font_info
documentation: Prints detailed font info.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_print.font_names
documentation: Prints the 'name' table and, if the font is CFF, the names in the 'CFF' table topDict.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_print.fonts_list
documentation: Prints a list of fonts with basic information.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_print.tbl_os2
documentation: Prints the OS/2 table.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_print.vf_instances
documentation: Prints a table of the named instances of a variable font.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_metrics.set_linegap
documentation:
Modifies the line spacing metrics in one or more fonts.

This is a fork of font-line by Source Foundry: https://github.com/source-foundry/font-line

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_metrics.align
documentation:
Aligns all fonts stored in INPUT_PATH folder to the same baseline.

To achieve this, the script finds the maximum ascender and the minimum descender values of the fonts stored in the
INPUT_PATH folder and applies those values to all fonts.

This can produce undesired effects (an exaggerated line height) when one or more fonts contain swashes, for example.
In such cases, it's better to copy the vertical metrics from a template font to one or more destination fonts using
the 'ftcli metrics copy-metrics' command.

See https://kltf.de/download/FontMetrics-kltf.pdf for more information.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_metrics.copy_metrics
documentation: Copies vertical metrics from a source font to one or more destination fonts.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_ttf.autohint
documentation: Autohint TrueType fonts using ttfautohint-py.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_ttf.decompose
documentation: Decompose composite glyphs of a TrueType font.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_ttf.dehint
documentation:
Drops hinting from TrueType fonts.

This is a CLI for dehinter by Source Foundry: https://github.com/source-foundry/dehinter

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_ttf.fix_contours
documentation: Fix winding and remove overlaps and tiny paths from contours.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_ttf.remove_overlaps
documentation: Simplify glyphs in TrueType fonts by merging overlapping contours.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_ttf.scale_upm
documentation:
Change the units-per-EM of TrueType fonts.

Hinting is removed from scaled TrueType fonts to avoid bad results. You may consider to use 'ftcli utils
ttf-autohint' to hint the scaled fonts.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_ttf.rename_glyph
documentation: Renames a glyph.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_name.set_name
documentation:
Adds a NameRecord to the name table. If a NameRecord with the same nameID, platformID and languageID already exists,
it will be overwritten.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_name.del_names
documentation:
Deletes the specified NameRecords from the name table. If no platformID is specified, all NameRecords with the
specified nameID will be deleted. If no language string is specified, all NameRecords with the specified nameID and
platformID will be deleted.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_name.find_replace
documentation:
Finds and replaces a string in the specified NameRecords. If no nameID is specified, the string will be replaced in
all NameRecords.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_name.del_mac_names
documentation:
Deletes all the Macintosh NameRecords (platformID=1) from the name table, except the ones with nameID 1, 2, 4, 5,
and 6.

According to Apple (https://developer.apple.com/fonts/TrueType-Reference-Manual/RM06/Chap6name.html), "names with
platformID 1 were required by earlier versions of macOS. Its use on modern platforms is discouraged. Use names with
platformID 3 instead for maximum compatibility. Some legacy software, however, may still require names with
platformID 1, platformSpecificID 0".

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_name.append
documentation:
Appends a prefix and/or a suffix to the specified NameRecords. If no platformID is specified, the prefix/suffix will
be added to all NameRecords with the specified nameID. If no language string is specified, the prefix/suffix will be
added to all NameRecords with the specified nameID and platformID.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_converter.ttf2otf
documentation:
Converts TTF fonts to OTF (or TrueType flavored woff/woff2 web fonts to PostScript flavored
woff/woff2 web fonts).

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_converter.otf2ttf
documentation:
Converts OTF fonts to TTF (or PostScripts flavored woff/woff2 web fonts to TrueType flavored
woff/woff2 web fonts).

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_converter.vf2i
documentation: Exports static instances from variable fonts.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_converter.wf2ft
documentation: Converts web fonts (WOFF and WOFF2) to SFNT fonts (TTF or OTF)

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_converter.ft2wf
documentation: Converts SFNT fonts (TTF or OTF) to web fonts (WOFF and/or WOFF2)

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_converter.ttc2sfnt
documentation: Extracts each font from a TTC file, and saves it as a TTF or OTF file.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_assistant.init
documentation:
Creates the `styles_mapping.json` and the `fonts_data.csv` files in the `ftCLI_files`
directory. If one or both files already exist, user will be prompted for overwrite.

Both files can be edited manually or using the `ftcli assistant ui` command.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_assistant.commit
documentation: Writes data from CSV to fonts.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_assistant.ui
documentation: Opens the character user interface to edit the styles_mapping.json and fonts_data.csv files.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_otf.autohint
documentation: Auto-hint OTF and PostScript flavored WOFF/WOFF2 fonts.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_otf.dehint
documentation: Remove hints from CFF flavored (OTF, WOFF, WOFF2) fonts.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_otf.fix_contours
documentation: Fix contours by correcting contours direction, removing overlaps and tiny paths.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_otf.fix_version
documentation:
Aligns CFF topDict version string to the head.fontRevision value.

For example, if head.fontRevision value is 2.001, CFF topDict version value will be 2.1.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_otf.subr
documentation: Subroutinize OpenType-PS fonts.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_otf.desubr
documentation: Desubroutinize OpenType-PS fonts.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_otf.check_outlines
documentation: Performs outline quality checks and overlaps removal with checkoutlinesufo.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_post.cli
documentation: A command line tool to manipulate the 'post' table.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_os2.recalc_x_height
documentation: Recalculates sxHeight value.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_os2.recalc_cap_height
documentation: Recalculates sCapHeight value.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_os2.recalc_avg_width
documentation: Recalculates xAvgCharWidth value.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_os2.recalc_max_context
documentation: Recalculates usMaxContext value.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_os2.recalc_ranges
documentation:
Generates a temporary Type 1 from the font file using tx, converts that to an OpenType font using makeotf, reads the
Unicode ranges and codepage ranges from the temporary OpenType font file, and then writes those ranges to the
original font's OS/2 table.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_os2.set_flags
documentation:
Sets/clears the following flags in OS/2.fsSelection and OS/2.fsType fields:

fsSelection:

 
Bit 0: ITALIC
Bit 1: UNDERSCORE
Bit 2: NEGATIVE
Bit 3: OUTLINED
Bit 4: STRIKEOUT
Bit 5: BOLD
Bit 6: REGULAR
Bit 7: USE_TYPO_METRICS
Bit 8: WWS
Bit 9: OBLIQUE

fsType:

 
Bits 0-3: Usage permissions
Bit 8: No subsetting
Bit 9: Bitmap embedding only

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_os2.set_version
documentation: Upgrades OS/2 table version.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_os2.panose
documentation: Command line panose editor.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_os2.set_attrs
documentation: Command line OS/2 table editor.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_hhea.cli
documentation: A command line tool to manipulate the `hhea` table.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_fix.empty_notdef
documentation:
Fix empty .notdef glyph.

Draw a simple rectangle to fill the empty .notdef glyph.

Rationale:

Glyph 0 must be assigned to a .notdef glyph. The .notdef glyph is very important for providing
the user feedback that a glyph is not found in the font. This glyph should not be left without
an outline as the user will only see what looks like a space if a glyph is missing and not be
aware of the active font’s limitation.

Fixing procedure:

* Draw a simple rectangle to fill the empty .notdef glyph

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_fix.monospace
documentation:
Fix metadata in monospaced fonts

fontbakery check id: com.google.fonts/check/monospace

Rationale:

There are various metadata in the OpenType spec to specify if a font is monospaced or not. If

: the font is not truly monospaced, then no monospaced metadata should be set (as sometimes they
  mistakenly are...)

Requirements for monospace fonts:

- `post.isFixedPitch` - "Set to 0 if the font is proportionally spaced, non-zero if the font

is not proportionally paced (monospaced)" (<https://www.microsoft.com/typography/otspec/post.htm>)

- `hhea.advanceWidthMax` must be correct, meaning no glyph's width value is greater.

(<https://www.microsoft.com/typography/otspec/hhea.htm>)

- `OS/2.panose.bProportion` must be set to 9 (monospace) on latin text fonts.
- `OS/2.panose.bSpacing` must be set to 3 (monospace) on latin handwritten or latin symbol

fonts.

- Spec says: "The PANOSE definition contains ten digits each of which currently describes up to sixteen variations.

Windows uses `bFamilyType`, `bSerifStyle` and `bProportion` in the font mapper to determine family type. It
also uses `bProportion` to determine if the font is monospaced."
(<https://www.microsoft.com/typography/otspec/os2.htm#pan>, <https://monotypecom-test.monotype.de/services/pan2>)

- `OS/2.xAvgCharWidth` must be set accurately. "OS/2.xAvgCharWidth is used when rendering monospaced fonts, at

least by Windows GDI" (<https://typedrawers.com/discussion/comment/15397/#Comment_15397>)

- `CFF.cff.TopDictIndex[0].isFixedPitch` must be set to `True` for CFF fonts.

Fixing procedure:

If the font is monospaced, then:

- Set `post.isFixedPitch` to `True` (1)
- Correct the `hhea.advanceWidthMax` value
- Set the `OS/2.panose.bProportion` value to 9 or 3, according to the `OS/2.panose.bFamilyType` value
- Set `CFF.cff.TopDictIndex[0].isFixedPitch` to `True` for CFF fonts

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_fix.nbsp_width
documentation:
Fix whitespace widths.

fontbakery check id: com.google.fonts/check/whitespace_widths

Rationale:

If the `space` and `nbspace` glyphs have different widths, then Google Workspace has problems with the font.

The `nbspace` is used to replace the space character in multiple situations in documents; such as the space before
punctuation in languages that do that. It avoids the punctuation to be separated from the last word and go to next
line.

This is automatic substitution by the text editors, not by fonts. It's also used by designers in text composition
practice to create nicely shaped paragraphs. If the `space` and the `nbspace` are not the same width, it breaks
the text composition of documents.

Fixing procedure:

- Check if `nbspace` and space glyphs have the same width. If not, correct `nbspace` width to match the

`space` width.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_fix.italic_angle
documentation:
Fix italic angle and italic/oblique bits.

fontbakery check ids:

 
\* com.google.fonts/check/italic_angle
\* com.google.fonts/check/caret_slope

Rationale:
The `post` table `italicAngle` property should be a reasonable amount, likely not more than 30°. Note that in
the OpenType specification, the value is negative for a rightward lean.

<https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/typography/opentype/spec/post>

Checks whether `hhea.caretSlopeRise` and `hhea.caretSlopeRun` match with `post.italicAngle`.

For Upright fonts, you can set `hhea.caretSlopeRise` to 1 and `hhea.caretSlopeRun` to 0.

For Italic fonts, you can set `hhea.caretSlopeRise` to `head.unitsPerEm` and calculate `hhea.caretSlopeRun`
like this:

`round(math.tan(math.radians(-1 * font["post"].italicAngle)) * font["head"].unitsPerEm)`

This check allows for a 0.1° rounding difference between the Italic angle as calculated by the caret slope and
`post.italicAngle`

Fixing procedure:

- Recalculate `post.italicAngle`
- Recalculate `hhea.caretSlopeRise` and `hhea.caretSlopeRun` according to the calculated `post.italicAngle`
- Set/clear the italic and/or oblique bits according to the calculated values
- In CFF fonts, recalculate also `CFF.topDictIndex[0].ItalicAngle` and set it to `post.italicAngle` rounded to

the nearest integer

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_fix.nbsp_missing
documentation:
Fix missing whitespace glyphs.

fontbakery check id: com.google.fonts/check/whitespace_glyphs

Rationale:

Font contains glyphs for whitespace characters?

Fixing procedure:

- Add a glyph for the missing `nbspace` character by double mapping the `space` character

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_fix.decompose_transformed
documentation:
Decompose glyphs with transformed components.

fontbakery check id: com.google.fonts/check/transformed_components

Rationale:

Some families have glyphs which have been constructed by using transformed components e.g. the 'u' being constructed
from a flipped 'n'.

From a designers point of view, this sounds like a win (less work). However, such approaches can lead to
rasterization issues, such as having the 'u' not sitting on the baseline at certain sizes after running the font
through ttfautohint.

Other issues are outlines that end up reversed when only one dimension is flipped while the other isn't.

As of July 2019, Marc Foley observed that ttfautohint assigns cvt values to transformed glyphs as if they are not
transformed and the result is they render very badly, and that vttLib does not support flipped components.

When building the font with fontmake, the problem can be fixed by adding this to the command line:

`--filter DecomposeTransformedComponentsFilter`

Fixing procedure:

- Decompose composite glyphs that have transformed components.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_fix.duplicate_components
documentation:
Remove duplicate components.

fontbakery check id: com.google.fonts/check/glyf_non_transformed_duplicate_components

Rationale:

There have been cases in which fonts had faulty double quote marks, with each of them containing two single quote
marks as components with the same x, y coordinates which makes them visually look like single quote marks.

This check ensures that glyphs do not contain duplicate components which have the same x,y coordinates.

Fixing procedure:

* Remove duplicate components which have the same x,y coordinates.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_fix.kern_table
documentation:
Fix `kern` table.

fontbakery check id: com.google.fonts/check/kern_table

Rationale:

Even though all fonts should have their kerning implemented in the `GPOS` table, there may be kerning info at the
`kern` table as well.

Some applications such as MS PowerPoint require kerning info on the kern table. More specifically, they require a
format 0 kern subtable from a kern table version 0 with only glyphs defined in the `cmap` table, which is the only
one that Windows understands (and which is also the simplest and more limited of all the kern subtables).

Google Fonts ingests fonts made for download and use on desktops, and does all web font optimizations in the serving
pipeline (using libre libraries that anyone can replicate.)

Ideally, TTFs intended for desktop users (and thus the ones intended for Google Fonts) should have both `kern` and
`GPOS` tables.

Given all of the above, we currently treat kerning on a v0 `kern` table as a good-to-have (but optional) feature.

Fixing procedure:

- Remove glyphs that are not defined in the `cmap` table from the `kern` table.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_fix.strip_names
documentation:
Remove leading and trailing spaces from `name` table entries.

fontbakery check id: com.google.fonts/check/name/trailing_spaces

Rationale:

Name table records must not have trailing spaces.

Fixing procedure:

- Remove leading and trailing spaces from all NameRecords in the `name` table.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_fix.empty_names
documentation:
Remove empty NameRecords from `name` table.

fontbakery check id: com.adobe.fonts/check/name/empty_records

Rationale:

Check name table for empty records.

Fixing procedure:

- Remove empty NameRecords from the `name` table.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_cff.del_names
documentation: Deletes CFF names in topDict.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_cff.set_names
documentation:
Sets CFF names in topDict. If the name is not present, it will be added. If the name is present, it will be
replaced.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_cff.find_replace
documentation:
Finds a string in the following items of CFF table topDict and replaces it with a new string: `version`, `FullName`,
`FamilyName`, `Weight`, `Copyright`, `Notice`.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_utils.add_dsig
documentation: Adds a dummy DSIG table to the fonts, unless the table is already present, or the font flavor is woff2.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_utils.del_table
documentation: Deletes the specified tables from the fonts.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_utils.font_organizer
documentation: Sorts fonts in folders by family name and optionally by foundry, font revision and extension.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_utils.font_renamer
documentation: Rename font files according to the provided source string.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_utils.rebuild
documentation: Rebuilds fonts by converting to XML and then converting back to the original format

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_utils.remove_unreachable_glyphs
documentation: Subsets a font file by removing unreachable glyphs.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.CLI.ftcli_utils.set_revision
documentation:
Sets [head].fontRevision and CFF.cff.topDictIndex[0].version values.

Optionally, also nameID 3 (Unique identifier) and nameID 5 (Version string) can be recalculated by using
`--unique-identifier` and `--version-string options`. Even if Unique identifier and Version string should be changed
according to the new version, they are optional to leave control to the user, who could choose to set those names
manually with ftcli name set-name or ftcli name find-replace commands.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.head.TableHead.get_font_revision
documentation:
Returns the 'fontRevision' value in 'head' table

```
return

a float representing the font revision
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.head.TableHead.set_font_revision
documentation:
Sets 'fontRevision' value in 'head' table

```
param value

a float representing the font revision
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.name.TableName.add_name
documentation:
Adds a NameRecord to the `name` table. If the NameRecord already exists, it will be overwritten.

```
param font

the font object

param string

The string to be written to the NameRecord

param name_id

The name ID of the NameRecord to be added

type name_id

int

param platform_id

1 = Macintosh, 3 = Windows

type platform_id

int

param language_string

The language of the string, defaults to en

type language_string

str (optional)
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.name.TableName.del_names
documentation:
Deletes all name records that match the given name_ids, optionally filtering by platform_id and/or
language_string.

```
param name_ids

A list of name IDs to delete

param platform_id

The platform ID of the name records to delete

param language_string

The language of the name records to delete
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.name.TableName.filter_namerecords
documentation:
It takes a list of name records, and returns a list of name records that match the given criteria

```
param name_ids

A list of name IDs to filter by

param platform_id

The platform ID of the name record

param plat_enc_id

The platform-specific encoding ID

param lang_id

The language ID of the name record

param lang_string

The string to search for in the name records

return

A list of name records.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.name.TableName.find_replace
documentation:
This function will find and replace a string in the specified namerecords.

```
param old_string

The string to be replaced

type old_string

str

param new_string

The string to replace the old string with

type new_string

str

param name_ids_to_include

A tuple of nameIDs to include in the search. If left blank, all nameIDs will be
included

type name_ids_to_include

tuple

param name_ids_to_skip

A tuple of nameIDs to skip in the search. If left blank, no nameID will be skipped

type name_ids_to_skip

tuple

param platform_id

The platform ID of the name record to be changed

type platform_id

int
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.name.TableName.append_string
documentation:
Appends a prefix, a suffix, or both to the namerecords that match the name IDs, platform ID, and language
string.

```
param name_ids

A list of name IDs to filter by

param platform_id

The platform ID of the namerecords where to append/prepend the string

param language_string

The language string to filter by

param prefix

The string to be added to the beginning of the namerecords

param suffix

The string to append to the end of the namerecords
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.CFF_.TableCFF.get_font_version
documentation:
Returns the cff.topDictIndex[0].version value

```
return

a string representing the cff.topDictIndex[0].version
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.CFF_.TableCFF.set_font_version
documentation:
Sets the cff.topDictIndex[0].version value

```
param value

a string representing the new cff.topDictIndex[0].version
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.CFF_.TableCFF.del_top_dict_names
documentation:
Deletes names from topDictIndex[0]

```
param names

a list of names
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.CFF_.TableCFF.get_fb_ps_name
documentation:
Returns the psName to pass to FontBuilder

```
return

a string representing the font's ps name
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.tables.CFF_.TableCFF.get_fb_font_info
documentation:
Returns the fontInfo dict to pass to FontBuilder

```
return

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.ps_recalc_zones.get_glyph_bounds
documentation:
Get the bounds of a glyph.

Parameters:
    font (TTFont): The TTFont object.
    glyph_name (str): The name of the glyph.

Returns:
    GlyphBounds: The bounds of the glyph.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the glyph does not exist in the font.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.ps_recalc_zones.get_glyph_bounds_many
documentation:
Get the bounds of multiple glyphs in a given font.

Parameters:
    font (TTFont): The TTFont object.
    glyph_names (List[str]): A list of glyph names.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary containing glyph names as keys and GlyphBounds objects as values.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.ps_recalc_zones.get_pair
documentation:
Get the two most common elements from the given counter.

Parameters:
    counter (Counter): The counter object containing elements and their counts.

Returns:
    List[float]: List containing the pair of most common elements.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.ps_recalc_zones.lists_overlaps
documentation:
Check if there are overlapping intervals in a list of lists.

Args:
    lists (List[List[float]]): A list of lists, where each inner list represents an interval.

Returns:
    bool: True if there are overlapping intervals, False otherwise.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.ps_recalc_zones.fix_lists_overlaps
documentation:
Fixes overlaps in a list of lists of floats.

Args:
    lists (List[List[float]]): A list of lists of floats.

Returns:
    List[List[float]]: The input list with any overlaps fixed.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.ps_recalc_zones.fix_min_separation_limits
documentation:
Fixes the minimum separation between zones.

Args:
    lists (List[List[float]]): A list of lists of floats.
    limit (int): The minimum separation between zones.

Returns:
    List[List[float]]: The input list with the minimum separation between zones fixed.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.ps_recalc_zones.calculate_zone
documentation:
Calculates the minimum and maximum vertical values for a given zone.

Parameters:
    font: TTFont object representing the font.
    glyph_names: List of glyph names to process.
    min_or_max: Literal specifying whether to process the minimum ('yMin') or maximum ('yMax')
        values.

Returns:
    List of float values representing the minimum or maximum vertical values for each glyph.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.ps_recalc_zones.recalc_zones
documentation:
Recalc Zones

Recalculates the zones for a given TTFont object.

Parameters:
    font (TTFont): The TTFont object.
    descender_glyphs (List[str]): A list of glyph names to use for calculating the descender
        zone.
    baseline_glyphs (List[str]): A list of glyph names to use for calculating the baseline
        zone.
    x_height_glyphs (List[str]): A list of glyph names to use for calculating the x-height
        zone.
    uppercase_glyphs (List[str]): A list of glyph names to use for calculating the uppercase
        zone.
    ascender_glyphs (List[str]): A list of glyph names to use for calculating the ascender
        zone.

Returns:
    Tuple[List[int], List[int]]: A tuple containing two lists. The first list contains the
        values for the OtherBlues zones, and the second list contains the values for the
        BlueValues zones.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.assistant.fonts_data.FontsData.__auto_shorten_name
documentation:
Tries to shorten a string below the maximum allowed len by replacing long words (e.g.: 'ExtraBold', 'Condensed')
with short ones (e.g.: 'XBd', 'Cn').

```
param string

The string to shorten

type string

str

param find_replace

a list of tuples, where the first element is the string to find (i.e.: the long word), and
the second element is the string to replace it with (i.e.: the short word)

type find_replace

list

param max_len

The maximum length of the string

type max_len

int

return

A string that is no longer than max_len characters.
```

to remove removed, and the words to keep kept. The returned string will be used to find a match and recalculate
data values.

```
param input_string

The string to remove words from

param remove_list

A list of words to be removed from the input string

param keep_list

A list of strings not to be removed from the input_string
```

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.ps_recalc_stems.get_stems
documentation:
Retrieves stem data from a font file for a given list of glyphs.

Parameters:
    file_path`: The path to the font file.
    glyph_list`: A list of glyph names for which stem data will be
    retrieved.

Returns:
    Tuple: a tuple containing two lists: the first list contains the horizontal stem data,
    and the second list contains the vertical stem data. Each list contains tuples of the
    form (stem width, stem count, glyph names).

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.ps_recalc_stems.recalc_stems
documentation:
Recalculates the StdHW and StdVW values for a given font file.

Parameters:
    file_path: A `Path` object representing the path to the file.
    h_stems_glyphs: A list of glyph names to use for calculating the horizontal stems.
    v_stems_glyphs: A list of glyph names to use for calculating the vertical stems.

Returns:
    A tuple containing the recalculated stem values for horizontal and vertical stems. The first
    value in the tuple represents the horizontal stem value, and the second value represents the
    vertical stem value.

bool = True, defaults to True

documentation:

Parameters:

    glyph_name (str): The name of the glyph.

Returns:
    bool: True if the glyph is empty.

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.cli_tools.get_files_in_path
documentation:
Get a list of files from a path. If the path is a directory, the function will return a list of all files found in
the directory. If `recursive` is True, the function will recursively search for files in subdirectories. If the
path is a file, the function will return a list containing only one file.
\:param input_path: path to the input file or directory
\:param recursive: If True, the function will recursively search for files in subdirectories
\:return: A list of pathlib.Path objects representing the files found in the path

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.cli_tools.get_variable_fonts_in_path
documentation:
Get a list of VariableFont objects from a path. If the path is a directory, the function will return a list of all
VariableFont objects found in the directory. If `recursive` is True, the function will recursively search for
VariableFonts in subdirectories. If the path is a file, the function will return a list containing only one
VariableFont.
\:param input_path: path to the input file or directory
\:param recursive: If True, the function will recursively search for VariableFonts in subdirectories
\:param recalc_timestamp: If True, the function `modified` timestamp in the `head` table will be recalculated
on save
\:return: A list of VariableFont objects

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.cli_tools.get_fonts_in_path
documentation:
Get a list of Font objects from a path, optionally filtering by extension and font type. If the path is a directory,
the function will return a list of all Font objects found in the directory. If `recursive` is True, the function
will recursively search for fonts in subdirectories. If the path is a file, the function will return a list
containing only one Font.

```
param input_path

The path to the input file or directory

param recursive

If True, the function will recursively search for fonts in subdirectories

param recalc_timestamp

If True, the modified timestamp in the head table will be recalculated on save

param allow_extensions

A list of allowed extensions.

param allow_ttf

If False, TrueType fonts will be excluded from the list

param allow_cff

If False, CFF fonts will be excluded from the list

param allow_static

If False, static fonts will be excluded from the list

param allow_variable

If False, variable fonts will be excluded from the list

param allow_web_font

If False, web fonts will be excluded from the list

param allow_sfnt

If False, sfnt fonts will be excluded from the list

return

A list of Font objects
```

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.cli_tools.initial_check_pass
documentation:
Checks if the list of fonts is not empty and if the output directory is writable.

```
param fonts

a list Font objects

param output_dir

the output directory

return

False if one of the checks fails, True if both checks succeed
```

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.cli_tools.get_project_files_path
documentation:
Get the path to the directory containing the project files (styles_mapping.json, fonts_data.csv).
\:param input_path:
\:return:

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.cli_tools.get_style_mapping_path
documentation:
Get the path to the styles_mapping.json file.
\:param input_path: Path to the input file or directory
\:return: A pathlib.Path object representing the styles_mapping.json file

functions.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.utils.cli_tools.get_fonts_data_path
documentation:
Get the path to the fonts_data.csv file.

classes.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_otf
documentation:
This function checks if the "CFF " table is present in the Font object and returns a boolean
value.

```
return

A boolean value indicating whether the font is OpenType-PS.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_ttf
documentation:
This function checks if the string "glyf" table is present in the Font object and returns a
boolean value.

```
return

A boolean value indicating whether the font is TrueType.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_variable
documentation:
This function checks if the "fvar" table is present in the Font object.

```
return

A boolean value indicating whether the font is variable.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_static
documentation:
The function checks if the "fvar" table is not present in the Font object.

```
return

A boolean value indicating whether the font is static.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_woff
documentation:
This function checks if the flavor of a font file is "woff" and returns a boolean value.

```
return

A boolean value indicating whether the font flavor is "woff" flavored or not.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_woff2
documentation:
This function checks if the flavor of a font file is "woff2" and returns a boolean value.

```
return

A boolean value indicating whether the font is "woff2" flavored or not.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_web_font
documentation:
This function checks if the font is in WOFF/WOFF2 format.

```
return

A boolean value indicating whether the "flavor" attribute of the object is None or
not. If it is None, the function will return False, otherwise it will return True.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_sfnt
documentation:
This function checks if the font is in SFNT format.

```
return

A boolean value indicating whether the "flavor" attribute of the object is None or
not. If it is None, the function will return True, otherwise it will return False.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_bold
documentation:
\> Checks if OS/2.fsSelection bit 5 and head.macStyle bit 0 are set

```
return

A boolean value.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_italic
documentation:
\> Checks if OS/2.fsSelection bit 0 and head.macStyle bit 1 are set

```
return

A boolean value.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_regular
documentation:
\> Checks if the fsSelection bit 6 is set, and the bold and italic bits are not set

```
return

A boolean value.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_oblique
documentation:
\> Checks if OS/2.fsSelection bit 9 is set

```
return

A bool value.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.is_upright
documentation:
If the font is not italic and not oblique, then it is upright

```
return

A bool value.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.created_timestamp
documentation:
This function returns the created timestamp stored in the 'head' table.

```
return

The function  is returning an integer value representing the
```

creation timestamp of the Font object.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.modified_timestamp
documentation:
This function returns the modified timestamp stored in the "head" table.

```
return

an integer value representing the modified timestamp of the Font object.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.set_bold_flag
documentation:
Sets the bold bit in the OS/2 table and the head table, and clears the regular bit in the
OS/2 table

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.set_italic_flag
documentation: Sets the italic bit in the OS/2 table and clears the regular bit

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.set_oblique_flag
documentation: Sets the oblique bit in the OS/2 table

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.set_regular_flag
documentation:
Sets the regular bit in the OS/2 table, clears the bold and italic bits in the OS/2 and head
tables

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.get_bounding_box
documentation: Returns max and min bbox of the font

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.set_created_timestamp
documentation:
This function sets the "created" attribute of the "head" table to the given timestamp.

```
param timestamp

an integer representing a Unix timestamp (number of seconds since January
1, 1970) that indicates when the font was created

type timestamp

int
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.set_modified_timestamp
documentation:
This function sets the "modified" attribute of the "head" table to the given timestamp.

```
param timestamp

an integer representing a Unix timestamp (number of seconds since January
1, 1970) that indicates when the font was last modified

type timestamp

int
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.get_real_extension
documentation:
This function returns the file extension of a font file based on its flavor or type.

```
return

A string representing the file extension of a font file. If the font has a flavor,
the extension will be ".woff" or ".woff2". If the font is a TrueType font, the extension
will be ".ttf". If the font is an OpenType font, the extension will be ".otf".
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.ttf_decomponentize
documentation: This function decomponentizes composite glyphs in a TrueType font.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.ttf_dehint
documentation: This function de-hints a TrueType font.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.ttf_remove_overlaps
documentation:
This function removes overlaps in a TrueType font while optionally ignoring errors and
removing hinting.

```
param remove_hinting

A boolean parameter that determines whether to remove hinting
information when removing overlaps in the font.

type remove_hinting

bool (optional)

param ignore_errors

If set to True, any errors encountered during the overlap removal
process will be ignored and the process will continue. If set to False, the process will
stop and raise an error if any issues are encountered, defaults to True

type ignore_errors

bool (optional)
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.ttf_scale_upem
documentation:
This function scales the UPM of a TrueType font. If the font is hinted, the hints will be
removed before scaling the UPM.

Args:
    units_per_em (int, optional): The target UPM. Defaults to 1000.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the font is not a TrueType font.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.otf_dehint
documentation: This function removes hints from the CFF table of an OpenType font.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.otf_desubroutinize
documentation: This function removes subroutines from an OpenType font.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.otf_subroutinize
documentation: This function subroutinizes an OpenType font.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.otf_recalc_zones
documentation: This function recalculates the zones of an OpenType font.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.otf_recalc_stems
documentation: This function recalculates the stems of an OpenType font.

documentation:
Set zones for a font.

```
param other_blues

Other blues.

param blue_values

Blue values.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.set_stems
documentation:
Set stems for a font.

```
param std_h_w

StdHW.

StdVW.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.add_dummy_dsig
documentation: This function adds a dummy DSIG table to a font file if it does not already exist.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.guess_family_name
documentation:
This function returns the font's family name, obtained from nameID 21, 16 or 1.

```
return

A string representing the font's family name.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.guess_subfamily_name
documentation:
This function returns the font's subfamily name, obtained from nameID 22, 17 or 2.

```
return

A string representing the font's subfamily name.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.get_file_name
documentation:
Returns the font's file name according to the passed source.

1: FamilyName-StyleName (FamilyName is retrieved from nameID 21, 16 or 1; SubFamily name
from nameID 22, 17 or 2)

2: PostScript Name

3: Full Font Name

4: CFF TopDict fontNames. Valid for CFF fonts only. For TTF files will be used '1' as
fallback value.

5: CFF TipDict FullName (returns: Family Name Style Name or FamilyName-StyleName, depending
on how FullName has been built). Valid for CFF fonts only. For TTF files will be used '1' as
fallback value.

```
param source

The NameRecord or combination of NameRecords from which to build the file
name.

return

A string representing the file name of the font.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.get_axes
documentation: This function returns a list of axes in a variable font.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.guess_foundry_name
documentation:
This function returns the foundry name of a font by checking the "name" or "OS/2" table.

```
return

a string that represents the foundry name. If nameID 8 is present in the "name"
table, the Manufacturer Name string is returned. If nameID 8 is not present, but nameID
9 is found, the Designer string is returned. If nor nameID 8 mor nameID 9 are present,
the vendor code from the font's OS/2 table is returned. If also the vendor code is empty
or not found, "Unknown" is returned.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.get_ui_name_ids
documentation:
Returns a list of all the UI name IDs in the font's GSUB table

```
return

A list of integers.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.reorder_ui_name_ids
documentation: Takes the IDs of the UI names in the name table and reorders them to start at 256

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.modify_linegap_percent
documentation:
Modifies the line spacing metrics

Adapted from https://github.com/source-foundry/font-line

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.get_font_info
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of font information

```
return

A dictionary of dictionaries.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.get_font_v_metrics
documentation:
The function returns a dictionary of dictionaries, where each dictionary contains a list of
dictionaries.

Each of the innermost dictionaries contains a label and a value.

The label is the name of the metric, and the value is the value of the metric.

The function returns the following metrics:

- OS/2 Typographic Ascender
- OS/2 Typographic Descender
- OS/2 Typographic Line Gap
- OS/2 Windows Ascent
- OS/2 Windows Descent
- hhea Ascent
- hhea Descent
- hhea Line Gap
- head Units Per Em
- head xMin
- head yMin
- head xMax
- head yMax
- head Font BBox

The function returns the metrics in a dictionary of dictionaries, where each dictionary
contains a list of dictionaries.

The innermost dictionaries contain a label
\:return: A dictionary with three keys: os2_metrics, hhea_metrics, and head_metrics.

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.get_font_feature_tags
documentation:
Returns a sorted list of all the feature tags in the font's GSUB and GPOS tables

```
return

A list of feature tags.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.calculate_italic_angle
documentation:
Calculates the italic angle of a font by drawing the glyph "H" and calculating the angle of
the slant.

```
param min_slant

The minimum slant angle that will be considered as italic, defaults to 2.0

type min_slant

float (optional)

return

An integer representing the italic angle of the font.
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.calculate_italic_bits
documentation:
If the italic angle is not 0, set the italic and/or oblique bits

```
param mode

click.IntRange(1, 3) = 1, defaults to 1

type mode

click.IntRange(1, 3) (optional)
```

methods.name: foundryToolsCLI.Lib.Font.Font.glyph_metrics_stats
documentation:
Returns a dict containing whether the font seems_monospaced,
what's the maximum glyph width and what's the most common width.

For a font to be considered monospaced, if at least 80% of ASCII
characters have glyphs, then at least 80% of those must have the same
width, otherwise all glyphs of printable characters must have one of
two widths or be zero-width.
Copied from fontbakery/profiles/shared_conditions.py
The Font class is a subclass of the fontTools.ttLib.TTFont class.
